testPrints.name: Tray-Slot Sliding Fit Pair
testPrints.reason: Tray exterior (103.2 × 94.2 mm) must slide smoothly into cradle slot (103.9 × 94.9 mm) with 0.35 mm per-side clearance. FDM dimensional error on a 103 mm part can reach ±0.2–0.3 mm — close enough to the clearance that a mis-scaled print could bind or rattle. This test print answers the question before committing to the full ~150 cm³ two-part print.
testPrints.simplification: Piece A: Y-slice of the cradle slot — two slot side walls (3.05 mm each) + 3 mm back wall + 4 mm base plate, 25 mm interior Y depth, open front and open top. Piece B: solid slug with the tray exterior cross-section (103.2 mm W × 30 mm H, r=3 corner fillets) extruded 25 mm in Y — no interior cavity, no ramp, no front lip. Both pieces placed on one bed with a 10 mm gap.
testPrints.verificationMethod: Trial fit: slide slug into channel. Smooth sliding with slight resistance = PASS. Binding = printer runs narrow, adjust XY compensation. Rattle = printer runs wide, adjust XY compensation.

testPrints.name: Printer Corner Fit (U-Test)
testPrints.reason: Cradle's printer pocket (80 × 154 mm interior) accommodates the PT-P750W (78 × 152 mm) with 1 mm/side XY clearance. Fit-reviewer verified the math, but FDM dimensional drift on a 78–152 mm part can reach ±0.2–0.3 mm — and the printer's actual corner radii/chamfers (not modeled in the proxy box) need to clear the cradle's sharp 90° interior corners. The U-shape covers three of the four pocket walls — both long sides + the closed back — so two inside back-corners and full-length engagement on both long axes are validated in one piece.
testPrints.matePart: Brother PT-P750W printer (78 × 152 × 143 mm) — user provides the actual printer, slides it into the open end (+Y) of the U so its back corners seat against the closed back wall
testPrints.simplification: U-shaped: three perpendicular walls reproducing the full pocket footprint at half cradle height. Closed back wall 86 × 3 × 12.5 mm (full outer X), two long side walls 154 × 3 × 12.5 mm each running the full pocket length along Y. Open at the front (−Y) for printer entry. No floor — U is rigid as printed; printer rests on the desk during fit. ~14.8 cm³, ~11% of full cradle volume. Half-height vs the full cradle wall (25 mm) cuts layer count and print time roughly in half despite tripling total wall length, because the printer's outline is a constant cross-section over its 143 mm height.
testPrints.verificationMethod: Place the U on a flat surface (any wall outside-face down). Slide the printer into the open end (+Y); printer should bottom out against the back wall with both long edges tracking parallel to the side walls. PASS = uniform ~1 mm gap to each side wall along the full 152 mm engagement, both back corners seat without binding. FAIL-tight = bump pocket clearance to 1.2 mm. FAIL-loose = tighten to 0.8 mm. FAIL-corner-bind = add inside-corner relief to printer pocket subtraction.

candidatesReviewed.feature: tray uniform low front wall + r=20 side fillet sweep
candidatesReviewed.rationale: Visual check on the real tray print. No functional tolerance at risk. Print reviewer confirmed PASS (marginal only at fillet corner thin wall, which the tray-slot-fit-pair print will also expose since it includes the full 30 mm tray height). No separate test piece warranted.

Print reviewer confirmed self-supporting in face-up orientation, all slope angles under 29° (well below 45° limit). No overhangs, no real bridges. Surface quality inside the tray is non-critical (labels rest on it). Verify in Bambu Studio slicer preview that no supports are generated; inspect ramp surface on the full tray print.

candidatesReviewed.disposition: SKIPPED — aesthetic, visible on full print
candidatesReviewed.rationale: Both fillet tiers passed review. The r=0.8 deviation on the front wall is documented-correct. Visual inspection on the first full tray print is sufficient.
Round 4 carry-over, verified in cradle-user-top-threequarter render every round since. No new geometry. Skip.

candidatesReviewed.rationale: $fn=200, 64 slab steps at ship quality — step height ~0.047 mm, below FDM resolution. Verified in renders. Inspect on full cradle print.

Purely vertical feature. No overhang. No tolerance requirement. Inspect on full cradle print.

The mini tray section (Piece B) includes the full 1.6 mm walls and r=3 corner fillets at full scale. If wall integrity is marginal, it will manifest in the test piece before the full print is committed. The r=20 fillet corner thin-wall finding (1.026 mm minimum, from print reviewer) is NOT included in the simplified slug, but is a cosmetic-only risk; visual inspection on the full tray print is sufficient.

fromPrintReviewer: tray r=20 fillet corner thin wall
